header.label: Transition Rate Analytics
header.headline: How many students move to the next stage? The drop at Middle → Secondary is the biggest gap.
header.sub: In 2021-22, most students successfully move between school stages — but the journey gets harder at each step. 98.6% make it from Foundational to Preparatory, 92.2% from Preparatory to Middle, and only 86.6% from Middle to Secondary. Girls consistently outperform boys at every stage.

cards.label: Stage 1: Foundational → Preparatory
cards.fullLabel: How many students move from Foundational to Preparatory school?
cards.headlineNote: Out of every 100 students in Foundational, this many reach Preparatory · 2021-22
cards.note: This is the easiest transition — nearly all students successfully move up from Class 2 to Class 3. Girls are slightly ahead of boys.

cards.label: Stage 2: Preparatory → Middle
cards.fullLabel: How many students move from Preparatory to Middle school (Class 6)?
cards.headlineNote: Out of every 100 students in Preparatory, this many reach Middle · 2021-22
cards.note: About 8 out of 100 students do not make it to Middle school. This is often linked to distance from school, cost pressures, and early dropouts.

cards.label: Girls vs Boys at the Hardest Stage
cards.fullLabel: Difference between girls and boys at Middle → Secondary transition
cards.headline: Boys +1.9 pts
cards.headlineNote: Girls are staying in school more than boys at this stage
cards.note: At the most difficult transition point, girls are actually doing better — 87.3% of girls move up vs 85.9% of boys. This is seen consistently across all three stages.

cards.label: Gap Between Social Groups
cards.fullLabel: Difference in Middle → Secondary transition between the highest and lowest social groups
cards.headline: 8.4 pts
cards.headlineNote: General students vs ST students at Middle → Secondary
cards.note: Scheduled Tribe (ST) students face the biggest barrier at Secondary entry — only 80.4% make it, compared to 91.6% for General category. This 11.2 point gap points to systemic inequality.

2021-22.chartTransClassSource: The chart below shows how many students (out of 100) move between each school stage. Each bar represents one transition: lower bars = more students are left behind at that step.
2021-22.chartTransClassNote: Stage 1 (Foundational → Preparatory): 98.6% · Stage 2 (Preparatory → Middle): 92.2% · Stage 3 (Middle → Secondary): 86.6%. The drop gets larger at each stage — most risk happens at the Secondary entry point.
2021-22.chartTransSocSource: This chart shows the Middle → Secondary transition rate broken down by social group — the stage where the largest gap between communities is visible. Each group shows all three transition stages.
2021-22.chartTransSocNote: At Middle → Secondary: General 91.6% · OBC 86.3% · SC 83.8% · ST 80.4%. The 11.2 point gap between General and ST students means that for every 100 ST students in Middle school, about 20 do not reach Secondary — compared to only 8 for General students.

insight1.label: Weakest Transition: Middle → Secondary
insight1.note: While 98.6% of students clear Stage 1 and 92.2% clear Stage 2, only 86.6% successfully move from Middle school to Secondary. This means roughly 13 out of every 100 students are lost at this single step — the most critical in India's school journey.
insight1.delta_label: Difference between the easiest and hardest transition stage

insight2.label: ST Students Face the Largest Gap
insight2.note: Among all social groups, Scheduled Tribe (ST) students have the lowest Middle → Secondary transition rate at 80.4%. General category students reach 91.6% — an 11.2 point gap. Closing this gap is critical to achieving equitable secondary education access.
insight2.delta_label: ST students reaching Secondary school — the lowest of all groups
